pet.name: Saw
pet.special_needs: Needs more oxygen than other fishes..
pet.characteristics: High-oxygen fish typically include active species such as goldfish, plecos, or fast-swimming cyprinids that require well-aerated, cooler water and frequent partial water changes. Provide strong filtration with adequate surface agitation, avoid overcrowding, and maintain appropriate tank volume (minimum 20 gallons recommended for most high-oxygen species). Monitor dissolved oxygen levels, water temperature, and pH regularly. Feed high-quality pellets or varied diet 1–2 times daily in appropriate portions. Enrichment includes plants, hiding spaces, and open swimming areas. Avoid sudden temperature fluctuations and ensure consistent water quality. Handle minimally; use nets only when necessary.
pet.species_profile_summary: Lifespan varies widely by specific species (3–10 years typical in captive settings); high-oxygen fish require robust aeration, frequent maintenance, and stable water conditions to thrive.

age_context.summary: Lifespan varies widely by specific species (3–10 years typical in captive settings); high-oxygen fish require robust aeration, frequent maintenance, and stable water conditions to thrive.

goal: Create a complete pet care plan with daily, weekly, monthly tasks, reminders, and safe condition-aware guidance.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: A senior pet plan should prioritize consistency over intensity. When time is limited, essential care such as feeding, hydration, medication reminders, and mobility support should be scheduled before optional enrichment.

retrieved_passages.title: Senior Pet Support
retrieved_passages.content: Older pets may benefit from routines that include medication reminders, slow feeding, and extra time for transitions. Owners should watch for changes in gait, confusion, appetite, and bathroom habits and report major changes to a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Kidney Diet and Hydration Support
retrieved_passages.content: Owners should follow the veterinary feeding plan already prescribed and avoid changing food or supplement amounts without veterinary guidance. If a pet refuses multiple meals or shows sudden decline, the safe action is to contact a veterinarian.

retrieved_passages.title: Medication Routine and Safety
retrieved_passages.content: Medication-related tasks in a home routine should focus on reminders, documentation, and observation, not dosage changes. A planning assistant can remind the owner to give medication exactly as already prescribed and to log whether the dose was completed.

retrieved_passages.title: Enrichment and Daily Monitoring
retrieved_passages.content: Observation tasks are useful for special-needs pets because they create a record of appetite, water intake, energy, and bathroom habits. These tasks should be short, specific, and easy to complete consistently.

raw_recommendations.name: Feed Saw high-quality pellets
raw_recommendations.notes: Provide appropriate portion size once daily. Monitor for appetite changes as part of senior pet routine.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets require consistent feeding schedules. High-oxygen fish benefit from regular, measured feeding to maintain health and allow monitoring of appetite.

raw_recommendations.name: Monitor Saw's activity and behavior
raw_recommendations.notes: Observe swimming patterns, energy level, and appetite. Note any changes in behavior or appearance. Keep brief daily log.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Observation tasks create a record of health status for senior pets. Changes in activity, appetite, or behavior should be documented and reported to a veterinarian if significant.

raw_recommendations.name: Check water aeration and oxygen levels
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify filter is running, check for adequate surface agitation, and observe for signs of oxygen stress (gasping at surface). Ensure strong aeration is active.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Saw requires more oxygen than typical fish. Daily verification of aeration equipment ensures dissolved oxygen remains adequate for a senior, high-oxygen fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Perform partial water change (25%)
raw_recommendations.notes: Remove 25% of tank water and replace with dechlorinated water at same temperature. High-oxygen fish require frequent partial changes to maintain water quality.
raw_recommendations.rationale: High-oxygen fish species depend on frequent partial water changes and consistent water conditions. Senior pets benefit from stable, well-maintained environments.

raw_recommendations.name: Clean or rinse filter media
raw_recommendations.notes: Rinse filter media in removed tank water (not tap water) to preserve beneficial bacteria. Ensure filter continues to provide strong aeration.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Strong filtration with adequate surface agitation is essential for high-oxygen fish. Regular maintenance prevents clogging and maintains oxygen exchange.

raw_recommendations.name: Test and record water parameters
raw_recommendations.notes: Test pH, temperature, and dissolved oxygen if possible. Record results. Maintain appropriate temperature and pH for species. Report significant deviations.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior high-oxygen fish require stable water conditions and consistent monitoring. Documentation helps identify trends and informs veterinary care decisions.

raw_recommendations.name: Inspect tank for hazards and maintain enrichment
raw_recommendations.notes: Verify hiding spaces and plants are secure. Ensure open swimming areas remain clear. Check for sharp edges or debris. Minimize handling.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Enrichment with plants and hiding spaces supports well-being. Regular inspection prevents injury and maintains a safe environment for a senior fish.

raw_recommendations.name: Deep clean tank and replace decorations as needed
raw_recommendations.notes: Monthly deep clean: remove and rinse decorations, vacuum substrate gently, clean tank walls. Avoid sudden temperature changes during cleaning.
raw_recommendations.rationale: Senior pets require consistent, stable environments. Monthly deep cleaning maintains water quality without the stress of frequent major disruptions.
